stem: A user types www.example.org into a browser and a page loads from a server on the internet.
model_answer: The Domain Name System (DNS) translates human-readable domain names into the IP addresses that computers need to locate a server. The browser asks a DNS resolver for the IP address of www.example.org; the resolver queries DNS servers until it finds the matching IP, then returns it to the browser. The browser then opens a TCP connection to that IP to request the web page.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Explain the role of DNS when the user requests this web page.

mark_points.text: DNS translates domain names into IP addresses
the browser sends the domain name to a DNS server/resolver
accepted_alternatives: computer queries DNS for the address

mark_points.text: the IP address is returned so the browser can connect to the correct web server
accepted_alternatives: browser uses the returned IP to request the page
